step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with agent1’s recommendation to perform UGI endoscopy. It provides direct visual confirmation of lesions within the gastrointestinal tract, including ulcers, erosions, or other abnormalities caused by chronic alcohol consumption. Furthermore, during this procedure, biopsies can be taken to rule out malignancy or confirm inflammatory processes associated with chronic gastritis or peptic ulcer disease. While CT enterography might also provide valuable anatomical details, its primary advantage over endoscopy lies more in assessing structural integrity rather than identifying functional issues directly related to mucosal health—especially given the specific findings here (epigastric pain, guarding). Thus, UGI endoscopy remains the optimal initial step towards diagnosing and managing this case effectively.
